macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market selloff wipes out about $86 billion as Bitcoin briefly drops to $74,255
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market fell sharply over the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin briefly sliding to $74,255 and major altcoins including Ethereum, Solana, XRP, and BNB also taking heavy losses. The broad selloff erased roughly $86 billion in market value.

items.title: Bitcoin struggles below $78,000 amid short-term selling and ETF outflows
items.summary: Bitcoin failed to reclaim the $78,000 level as selling pressure from short-term holders and heavy spot ETF offloading weighed on prices. The weakness also dampened momentum across the broader altcoin market.

items.title: Trump Media moves to sell Bitcoin as losses hit $455 million
items.summary: Trump Media reportedly filed plans to sell Bitcoin while the company’s losses climbed to $455 million. The move stands out as one of the day’s notable corporate crypto developments.

items.title: Senator Lummis says CLARITY Act would end crypto regulatory ambiguity
items.summary: Senator Cynthia Lummis said the CLARITY Act could resolve ongoing uncertainty in U.S. crypto regulation. The comment comes as lawmakers continue debating a framework for digital assets and market structure.

items.title: Crypto.com users advance lawsuit over third-party data collection
items.summary: Users of Crypto.com are moving forward with a lawsuit alleging improper third-party data collection. The case adds to the growing legal scrutiny facing major crypto platforms over privacy practices.

items.title: THORChain proposes recovery plan after May 15 exploit
items.summary: THORChain has put forward a recovery plan following the May 15 exploit and says no new RUNE will be minted as part of the response. The proposal is aimed at restoring confidence after the security incident.

items.title: XRP Ledger enters new phase with incoming update
items.summary: The XRP Ledger is moving into a new phase with an upcoming XRPL update, alongside reports of strong activity in the ecosystem. The development highlights continued project-level momentum even as the broader market weakens.

items.title: ICE and OKX bring oil perpetual contracts to crypto market
items.summary: Intercontinental Exchange and OKX launched oil perpetual contracts, expanding the range of traditional-market products available in crypto. The move reflects continued convergence between digital asset exchanges and legacy financial markets.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google previews Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini Omni and new AI stack at I/O 2026
items.summary: Google’s latest AI updates include the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash, expansion of the Gemini model lineup, and new tools spanning media generation, robotics, and agent workflows. The company also highlighted Gemini Robotics 1.6, Genie 3 world models, and new agent-building protocols across its ecosystem.

items.title: Microsoft MDASH harness reportedly beats Anthropic’s secret Mythos on cyber benchmark
items.summary: A weekly AI recap claims Microsoft’s MDASH harness outperformed Anthropic’s undisclosed Mythos model on a cybersecurity benchmark, underscoring the growing importance of evaluation harnesses and system design over raw model capability. The update also framed this as a sign that infrastructure and tooling can be as decisive as model size.

items.title: Anthropic reportedly closes massive funding round at $900 billion valuation
items.summary: A week-in-AI roundup says Anthropic raised $30 billion at a $900 billion valuation, one of the largest reported AI financings to date. The same update said Karpathy joined Anthropic and that the company continues expanding its enterprise and cloud footprint.

items.title: Google expands Gemini with agentic search, AI Studio features and Robotics 1.6
items.summary: Google’s AI showcase emphasized new end-to-end capabilities across models, multimodal generation, and robotics. The company highlighted agentic search, new video/image generation features, and broader availability of robotics and world-model tools for developers.

items.title: AI industry focus shifts toward harnesses, agents and tool use over raw model size
items.summary: Recent AI commentary highlighted a broader trend: better scaffolding, agent orchestration, and retrieval systems are increasingly seen as more important than merely scaling model parameters. The discussion also noted that RAG workflows are evolving beyond traditional vector databases toward hybrid and file-search approaches.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold foldable phone
items.summary: Samsung debuted its most advanced foldable yet, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame. The launch highlights continued innovation in premium consumer hardware beyond AI-focused products.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to scale Android from phones to desktops, aiming to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computing. The effort could reshape the PC market if the companies can deliver a compelling desktop experience.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 for wearables
items.summary: Qualcomm introduced its latest wearable platforms, focusing on better battery life, design flexibility, and connectivity. The update targets key barriers that have limited wider adoption of smartwatches and other wearables.

items.title: Quantum Motion deploys first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has built the first full-stack quantum computer using conventional silicon chip manufacturing techniques. The milestone could help accelerate the path toward scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is expanding Alexa+ beyond devices and onto the web as it competes more directly with other digital assistants and chatbot platforms. The move is part of a broader push toward more agentic workflows across devices.
